steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two events: NASA crashing a probe into Dimorphos, then Dimorphos's orbital period being permanently altered. The transition must signal that the second event is a direct result of the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Specifically,’: signals that the second statement narrows or gives a precise detail about the first. The orbital change is a consequence, not a specification. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In comparison,’: signals contrast between two different things. Nothing is being compared here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In addition,’: signals an extra, unrelated point being added. The orbital change is not an independent addition; it happened because of the impact. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Consequently,’: signals a direct cause-and-effect link. The probe impact caused the orbital change, so this transition fits exactly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
